claim: Every agent at domains/<name>/agents/<name>/ shall declare a non-empty PURPOSE describing what the agent stewards in one line (machine-readable, alongside its SCOPE).
why: An agent without a declared purpose is invisible to the operator-prompt -- the AGENT-MAP block cannot render its responsibility. A machine-readable PURPOSE placed next to SCOPE keeps the agent's contract in one place, distinct from the prose README. Not yet implemented: the agents/ scaffolding infrastructure (create-agent, scope/purpose declaration) is Planned, so no agent exists to declare a PURPOSE yet; the policy stands for the moment one is scaffolded.
